outOfScope.body: Coordination with the receiving POTW on any required updates to the brewery's industrial pretreatment permit as a result of system modifications is not included in this scoping document. The scope of permit coordination will be confirmed during detailed scoping.

outOfScope.heading: Potable water system modifications
outOfScope.body: Modifications to the brewery's potable water supply or distribution system are out of scope. The reuse system delivers non-potable quality water to a dedicated cooling tower makeup connection; the potable system remains unchanged.

outOfScope.heading: Brewing process water reuse beyond cooling tower makeup
outOfScope.body: Reuse of treated effluent for brewing process water (mash, sparge, cleaning) is not included in this scope. Phase-2 expansion of the reuse system for brewing process applications may be evaluated in a follow-on engagement.

gatesToClose.gate: Confirm projected daily average flow at 2× production from expansion planning documents
gatesToClose.closer: Sustainability director to provide from expansion planning; field agent to request at pre-committee working session

gatesToClose.gate: Obtain POTW surcharge schedule for high-strength BOD/TSS discharge
gatesToClose.closer: Sustainability director or POTW pretreatment coordinator; needed to finalise surcharge savings row

gatesToClose.gate: Confirm cooling tower makeup demand (gal/day) and current municipal water rate
gatesToClose.closer: Brewery operations team via sustainability director; needed to finalise reuse savings calculation

gatesToClose.gate: Confirm SBR tankage condition and reuse feasibility
gatesToClose.closer: Field agent site assessment during detailed scoping phase; determines MBR CAPEX range midpoint

finalText:
All four artifacts are ready. Here's the package summary.

---

## Full opportunity package — Regional Craft Brewery (Colorado Front Range)

**Stage: Scope | Confidence: MEDIUM**
**Bottom line: The SBR expansion looks cheaper at year 0 by $500K–$900K. Fully priced over 5 years — BOD surcharges at 2× loading, full potable water purchase unreduced, and probable retrofit exposure at the next growth cycle — the SBR path costs the brewery ~$500K–$1.5M more than the MBR retrofit while leaving them capacity-constrained again. The MBR path is the last pretreatment investment they make at this facility for a decade.**

---

### Your four PDFs

| Artifact | File |
|---|---|
| 📋 **Field Brief** | [regional-craft-brewery_field-brief.pdf](?threadId=eval-happy-path-brewery-mbr-retrofit-scoping-6W5k4KMMHK-oGx33BC1Z6&kind=field-brief) |
| 🎯 **Playbook** | [regional-craft-brewery_playbook.pdf](?threadId=eval-happy-path-brewery-mbr-retrofit-scoping-6W5k4KMMHK-oGx33BC1Z6&kind=playbook) |
| 📊 **Analytical Read** | [regional-craft-brewery_analytical-read.pdf](?threadId=eval-happy-path-brewery-mbr-retrofit-scoping-6W5k4KMMHK-oGx33BC1Z6&kind=analytical-read) |
| 📄 **Proposal Shell** | [regional-craft-brewery_proposal-shell.pdf](?threadId=eval-happy-path-brewery-mbr-retrofit-scoping-6W5k4KMMHK-oGx33BC1Z6&kind=proposal-shell) |

---

### Three things the Field Brief will tell you to do

1. **Contact the sustainability director this week** and propose a pre-committee working session. Your goal: get the 5-year cost framing into the deck the sustainability director presents to the ops committee — not as an attachment, but in the narrative. Also ask directly whether any other vendors are involved.

2. **Confirm four numbers** before you finalize the deliverable: projected flow at 2× production, the POTW's BOD/TSS surcharge schedule, cooling tower makeup demand in gal/day, and the current municipal water rate. These move two rows in the 5-year comparison from directional to defensible — and defensible numbers win committee votes.

3. **Deliver the scoping comparison before the committee meeting, not at it.** The Proposal Shell is your seed document — edit it and get it in the sustainability director's hands with enough lead time for their own prep. If a competing SBR expansion quote lands first, the anchor is set against you.

---

### One flag to carry into every conversation

**SPECIALIST:** The ops committee decision-makers haven't been in the room yet. The sustainability director is your champion, but this capex decision will be made by people who've only seen the summary. If the comparison reaches them as a year-0 CAPEX table, the SBR expansion wins. Your job this month is to make sure the committee sees the 5-year picture before that happens.
